name: Scindapsus 'Silver Hero'
aboutText: Scindapsus 'Silver Hero' is a refined climbing cultivar with elongated, lanceolate to elliptic leaves that display an exceptional satin-silver surface with subtle darker veining beneath. The leaves are unusually thick and matte to softly glossy, with a texture that catches light in a way that makes the silver colouration shift and shimmer as viewing angle changes. It vines readily with distinct nodes and visible aerial roots, reflecting its ancestry from the tropical climbing aroid forests of Southeast Asia. Considered one of the most elegant Scindapsus cultivars available, it appeals particularly to collectors who appreciate subtlety over bold variegation.

morphology.venation: Subtle, slightly darker than silver blade
morphology.texture: Thick, matte to softly glossy, satin-silver
morphology.variegation: Uniform silver surface — not sectoral variegation
morphology.growthHabit: Vining climber with visible aerial roots

careGuide.substrate: Well-draining mix: 40% perlite, 40% potting compost, 20% orchid bark.
careGuide.watering: Water when the top 3–4 cm is dry. These are generally forgiving and will tolerate slight drought better than overwatering.
careGuide.humidity: 50–70% preferred. Most varieties in this group are more adaptable to typical indoor conditions than true aroids.
careGuide.fertilising: Balanced liquid fertiliser at half strength every 2 weeks in spring and summer. Monthly in winter.
careGuide.repotting: Every 12–18 months when root-bound. These vining plants grow quickly and may need annual repotting in peak years.

commonProblems.fix: Allow to dry more between waterings; move to a brighter position

commonProblems.cause: Insufficient light causing reversion to green
commonProblems.fix: Move to a brighter position — variegated leaves need more light to maintain their pattern
Repot into fresh well-draining substrate; reduce watering frequency

items: Ensure there is at least one healthy node present — a cutting without a node cannot produce new growth
For variegated forms, check that the variegation pattern appears on the newest growth and shows no signs of reversion to green
Inspect the underside of leaves for spider mites (fine webbing or stippling) which are common on pothos-family plants
Confirm the cutting has visible roots or a healthy node before purchasing — unrooted cuttings require propagation experience to succeed

fieldNotes.title: Selected for Silver
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A cultivated Scindapsus selection with enhanced silver marking across the leaf surface — the 'Silver Hero' name indicating a selection for maximum silver coverage, which is the collector preference in Scindapsus generally. At accessible price tier. Whether Silver Hero represents a genuinely distinct cultivar with consistent selection criteria or a marketing denomination applied variably to well-silvered Scindapsus plants is a question the market has not fully resolved. The silver coverage is the appeal; the name does what it needs to do commercially.
